title: CAMbank: CPT Field Processing v1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fb2s0","text":"Field processing of CPT vacutainers for the Cornell Aerospace Medicine Biobank (CAMbank).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3tkje","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dmt7m","text":"Instructions for preserving: Plasma, PBMCs, and RBC Pellets.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ffm6c","text":"Tube Type: BD Vacutainer® Mononuclear Cell Preparation Tube (CPT) (BD Biosciences: #362753)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: After venipuncture, invert the tubes 8 to 10 times to fully mix in the sodium heparin anticoagulant. Store the tube upright at room temperature until centrifugation. To ensure proper barrier formation, blood samples should be centrifuged within 2 hours of blood collection. Centrifugation more than 2 hours after specimen collection may cause incomplete barrier formation.
Note: A swing bucket centrifuge is required. Set centrifuge: acceleration: 9 deceleration: 0 temperature: RT duration: 30 minutes speed: 1800xg

Place the CPTs in the centrifuge. Place a protective cover over the swing buckets in case of tube breakage. Start the centrifuge. Stand by the centrifuge until the centrifuge reaches max speed. Listen for signs of imbalance or compromised tube integrity.
Carefully remove CPTs from the centrifuge and inspect for separation of red blood vs PMBCs vs plasma layers. Transfer CPTs to a sanitized laminar flow hood.
Prepare the wash buffer (PBS + 2% FBS) In a 50mL conical tube, mix: 24.4mL of PBS 600µL of FBS Create more wash buffer as needed (depending on the number of tubes drawn).
Add 5mL PBS + 2% FBS to each CPT tube. Gently resuspend the peripheral blood mononucleocyte cells (PBMC) layer into the PBS + 2% FBS using a serological pipet.

Using a P1000, carefully aliquot plasma (yellow layer above buffy layer) into 2D barcoded tubes at 500µL each. Do not pipet close enough to disturb the buffy coat (the cloudy PBMC cell layer). Return 2D tubes with plasma to the rack and place in the -80C freezer.
One CPT tube at a time, transfer the resuspended cells into a new 15mL conical tube.
Add fresh wash buffer to bring the conical tube volume to 15mL.
Pellet the PBMCs in the centrifuge. Set centrifuge: acceleration: 9 deceleration: 9 temperature: RT duration: 15 minutes speed: 300xg
Return the 15mL conical tubes to the laminar flow hood. Gently aspirate the media away from the cell pellet.
Prepare freezing media: 10% DMSO, 90% FBS. One CPT will require 6mL of freezing media.
Resuspend the cells of each 15mL conical tube in 6mL of freezing media.
Transfer 1mL of PBMCs into each cryovial.
Place the cryovials into Mr. Frosty containers.
Store the Mr. Frosty's at -80C overnight, then ship back to WCM for liquid nitrogen storage.
Recap and freeze the CPT tubes to preserve DNA in the red blood cell pellet
creator: Eliah G Overbey
